reachability_artifacts: built sworn merge-track plus autonomous-loop and MCP calls over the same temporary Git fixture, recording identical parents/tree, idempotent re-gating, exact local-only recognized-sync invalidation, unchanged remote/release-wt refs, and fail-closed outcomes
release_benefit: Every way Sworn merges a track now enforces one deterministic and restart-safe integration contract.

design_decisions.choice: Use five dependency-safe tracks, serializing the public engine scaffold in T2 with exclusive cross-track file ownership and no shared-touchpoint exception in this release.
options: five tracks with serial engine bootstrap
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified under Brad's 2026-07-16 Coach instruction to follow the orchestrator recommendation: use the five-track graph and block for re-planning if any undeclared cross-track write appears.
design_decisions.rationale: The graph gives every foundational slice a real binary path, preserves the downstream parallel seam, and keeps every cross-track merge deterministic and collision-free.

positive_scenarios: CLI, autonomous-loop, and MCP produce the same canonical integration, or the same exact local-only invalidation commit for the sole recoverable recognized-sync case, and revalidate on rerun.
negative_scenarios: Later overlap, shipped/unrecognized provenance, stale report, invalid rollback, undeclared conflict, non-canonical shared blob, or rewritten result blocks every adapter without lifecycle or ref mutation.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Removing three divergent merge predicates eliminates the weakest-path bypass from automated delivery.
